meaning: adj.原则性强的,有(道德)原则的
examples: She was a strong, principled woman.
Earlier generations said they wanted their children to be like Lincoln: principled, kind, compassionate, resolute.
Lovelock's principled nonconformity can be traced to his childhood.
What is needed, says Will, is a coherent and principled way of dealing with this mess.
Chairman Martin really seems to think that he's taking a principled stand right now.
